Models
memberships
Schemas
powered by Zudoku
Open Education API
Open Education API

programme offerings

Endpointhttp://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6

The offerings API provides information about offerings which have a global timeframe, e.g. a period to which students can enrol.

GET /academic-sessions/{academicSessionId}/programme-offerings

GET
http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6
/academic-sessions/{academicSessionId}/programme-offerings

Get a list of all programme offerings during this academic session

GET /academic-sessions/{academicSessionId}/programme-offerings › path Parameters

academicSessionId
​string · uuid · required

Academic session ID

GET /academic-sessions/{academicSessionId}/programme-offerings › query Parameters

pageSize
​integer · enum · int32

The number of items per page

Enum values:
10
20
50
100
250
Default: 10
pageNumber
​integer · int32 · min: 1

The page number to get. Page numbers start at 1.

consumer
​string

Request entities intended for a specific consumer. The consumer profile allows for adding additional data, or specific rules concerning the presentation of the data. A consumer can be selected based on the key of the consumer profile. An implementation of the OEAPI SHOULD always return the consumer information inside the consumer property of the object(s) that are requested. Further information regarding the use of consumers can be found in the documentation

​object · style: deepObject · explode: true

Filter object serialised as filter_query[field][operation]=value. Multiple top-level fields are combined with AND. CSV is accepted where noted.

Inspired by Storyblok (https://www.storyblok.com/docs/api/content-delivery/v2/filter-queries)

Wildcards:

  • Prefer using only the asterisk * as a wildcard for partial matches (e.g., like and not_like).

Implementation note:

  • The availability and behaviour of this query functionality are entirely determined by the organisation hosting the API implementation. It is not mandatory for implementers to support this functionality, and it cannot be enforced upon organisations that provide or consume OEAPI endpoints.
  • It is up to each implementer to decide whether to support this feature. It is not a requirement of the OEAPI standard itself.
  • Consumers or working groups that wish to apply specific filtering mechanisms are encouraged to do so using this approach for the sake of consistency across implementations.

Examples:

  • Filter course offerings by programme, delivery, language and start date
    filter_query[programme.code][in]=B-IT-2025&filter_query[organisation.code][in]=RuG&filter_query[mode_of_delivery][in]=on_campus,hybrid&filter_query[language_of_instruction][in]=en-GB&filter_query[start_date][gt_date]=2025-09-01T00:00:00Z

  • Only offerings with email contact present
    filter_query[contacts.email][is]=not_empty

​object[] · style: deepObject · explode: true

OR blocks. Provide an array of single-field filter objects, each combined with OR. Serialises as filter_query[__or][][field][operation]=value.

Inspired by Storyblok (https://www.storyblok.com/docs/api/content-delivery/v2/filter-queries)

Wildcards:

  • Prefer using only the asterisk * as a wildcard for partial matches (e.g., like).

Implementation note:

  • The availability and behaviour of this query functionality are entirely determined by the organisation hosting the API implementation. It is not mandatory for implementers to support this functionality, and it cannot be enforced upon organisations that provide or consume OEAPI endpoints.
  • It is up to each implementer to decide whether to support this feature. It is not a requirement of the OEAPI standard itself.
  • Consumers or working groups that wish to apply specific filtering mechanisms are encouraged to do so using this approach for the sake of consistency across implementations.

Examples:

  • Provider is Org A or Org B, OR campus city contains “Utrecht”
    ?filter_query[__or][][organisation.id][in]=org-uu,org-hku&filter_query[__or][][campus.city][like]=*Utrecht*

  • Start date after 1 Sept 2025 OR has evening/block_week tag
    ?filter_query[__or][][start_date][gt_date]=2025-09-01T00:00:00Z&filter_query[__or][][tags][any_in_array]=evening,block_week

fields
​string · style: form

Allows clients to indicate which fields should be included in the response.
This parameter supports the principle of data minimisation and helps to optimise data usage and performance by reducing unnecessary data transmission.

The fields parameter uses nested field selection syntax with parentheses for subfields, for example: programme(code) or campus(city).
Multiple fields can be grouped within parentheses, for example:
fields=(id,title,ectsCredits,programme(code),campus(city)).

When omitted, the server returns all fields the client has access to.
Unknown field names SHOULD be ignored.
The server MUST always include mandatory fields (e.g., identifiers such as id) that are required for a valid or minimal response, even if not explicitly requested.

Important: This is a request hint, not a security feature.
The server MAY disregard the request for a restricted set of fields, and the final response structure MAY depend on server logic and the client’s access rights.

If a client requests unauthorised fields, these MUST be silently omitted or redacted.

q
​string

Filter by items having a name, abbreviation or description containing the given search term (exact partial match, case insensitive)

teachingLanguage
​string · minLength: 2 · pattern: ^([a-z]{2,3})(-([A-Z…

Filter by teachingLanguage, which is a string describing the main teaching language, should be at least a two-letter language code as specified by ISO 6467.

state
​string · enum

Filter by an Offering's state.

The state of this offering:

  • concept: The offering is still in development and not yet available for students
  • cancelled: The offering has been cancelled and is no longer available
  • active: The offering is currently available for students to enrol in and participate
  • inactive: The offering is not currently available for students, but may be available in the future

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
concept
cancelled
active
inactive
resultExpected
​boolean

Filter by resultExpected

since
​string · date-time

Filter all offerings by providing a minimum start moment for the corresponding academic session, RFC3339 (full-date). By default only future offerings are shown (equal to ?since=<today>).

until
​string · date-time

Filter all offerings by providing a maximum end moment for the corresponding academic session, RFC3339 (full-date).

GET /academic-sessions/{academicSessionId}/programme-offerings › Responses

OK

pageSize
​integer · int32 · required

The number of items per page

pageNumber
​integer · int32 · min: 1 · required

The current page number

hasPreviousPage
​boolean · required

Whether there is a previous page

hasNextPage
​boolean · required

Whether there is a previous page

​object[] · required
totalPages
​integer | null · int32

Total number of pages

ext
​object

Object for additional non-standard attributes

GET/academic-sessions/{academicSessionId}/programme-offerings
curl --request GET \ --url http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6/academic-sessions/:academicSessionId/programme-offerings
shell
Example Responses
{ "pageSize": 10, "pageNumber": 1, "hasPreviousPage": false, "hasNextPage": true, "totalPages": 8, "items": [ { "programmeOfferingId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-134564174000", "primaryCode": { 2020" } ], "abbreviation": "SPRING-2026", "startDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "endDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [], "language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of ­­­statistics, including:\nHandle hypothesis testing with complex designs (e.g., including , dependent, independent, and co variates)\nData preparation (e.g., coding and feature selection)\nReason towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": [], "modesOfDelivery": [], "maxNumberStudents": 200, json
application/json

GET /organisations/{organisationId}/programme-offerings

GET
http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6
/organisations/{organisationId}/programme-offerings

Get a list of all programme offerings for a given organisation

GET /organisations/{organisationId}/programme-offerings › path Parameters

organisationId
​string · uuid · required

Organisation ID

GET /organisations/{organisationId}/programme-offerings › query Parameters

pageSize
​integer · enum · int32

The number of items per page

Enum values:
10
20
50
100
250
Default: 10
pageNumber
​integer · int32 · min: 1

The page number to get. Page numbers start at 1.

consumer
​string

Request entities intended for a specific consumer. The consumer profile allows for adding additional data, or specific rules concerning the presentation of the data. A consumer can be selected based on the key of the consumer profile. An implementation of the OEAPI SHOULD always return the consumer information inside the consumer property of the object(s) that are requested. Further information regarding the use of consumers can be found in the documentation

​object · style: deepObject · explode: true

Filter object serialised as filter_query[field][operation]=value. Multiple top-level fields are combined with AND. CSV is accepted where noted.

Inspired by Storyblok (https://www.storyblok.com/docs/api/content-delivery/v2/filter-queries)

Wildcards:

  • Prefer using only the asterisk * as a wildcard for partial matches (e.g., like and not_like).

Implementation note:

  • The availability and behaviour of this query functionality are entirely determined by the organisation hosting the API implementation. It is not mandatory for implementers to support this functionality, and it cannot be enforced upon organisations that provide or consume OEAPI endpoints.
  • It is up to each implementer to decide whether to support this feature. It is not a requirement of the OEAPI standard itself.
  • Consumers or working groups that wish to apply specific filtering mechanisms are encouraged to do so using this approach for the sake of consistency across implementations.

Examples:

  • Filter course offerings by programme, delivery, language and start date
    filter_query[programme.code][in]=B-IT-2025&filter_query[organisation.code][in]=RuG&filter_query[mode_of_delivery][in]=on_campus,hybrid&filter_query[language_of_instruction][in]=en-GB&filter_query[start_date][gt_date]=2025-09-01T00:00:00Z

  • Only offerings with email contact present
    filter_query[contacts.email][is]=not_empty

​object[] · style: deepObject · explode: true

OR blocks. Provide an array of single-field filter objects, each combined with OR. Serialises as filter_query[__or][][field][operation]=value.

Inspired by Storyblok (https://www.storyblok.com/docs/api/content-delivery/v2/filter-queries)

Wildcards:

  • Prefer using only the asterisk * as a wildcard for partial matches (e.g., like).

Implementation note:

  • The availability and behaviour of this query functionality are entirely determined by the organisation hosting the API implementation. It is not mandatory for implementers to support this functionality, and it cannot be enforced upon organisations that provide or consume OEAPI endpoints.
  • It is up to each implementer to decide whether to support this feature. It is not a requirement of the OEAPI standard itself.
  • Consumers or working groups that wish to apply specific filtering mechanisms are encouraged to do so using this approach for the sake of consistency across implementations.

Examples:

  • Provider is Org A or Org B, OR campus city contains “Utrecht”
    ?filter_query[__or][][organisation.id][in]=org-uu,org-hku&filter_query[__or][][campus.city][like]=*Utrecht*

  • Start date after 1 Sept 2025 OR has evening/block_week tag
    ?filter_query[__or][][start_date][gt_date]=2025-09-01T00:00:00Z&filter_query[__or][][tags][any_in_array]=evening,block_week

fields
​string · style: form

Allows clients to indicate which fields should be included in the response.
This parameter supports the principle of data minimisation and helps to optimise data usage and performance by reducing unnecessary data transmission.

The fields parameter uses nested field selection syntax with parentheses for subfields, for example: programme(code) or campus(city).
Multiple fields can be grouped within parentheses, for example:
fields=(id,title,ectsCredits,programme(code),campus(city)).

When omitted, the server returns all fields the client has access to.
Unknown field names SHOULD be ignored.
The server MUST always include mandatory fields (e.g., identifiers such as id) that are required for a valid or minimal response, even if not explicitly requested.

Important: This is a request hint, not a security feature.
The server MAY disregard the request for a restricted set of fields, and the final response structure MAY depend on server logic and the client’s access rights.

If a client requests unauthorised fields, these MUST be silently omitted or redacted.

q
​string

Filter by items having a name, abbreviation or description containing the given search term (exact partial match, case insensitive)

teachingLanguage
​string · minLength: 2 · pattern: ^([a-z]{2,3})(-([A-Z…

Filter by teachingLanguage, which is a string describing the main teaching language, should be at least a two-letter language code as specified by ISO 6467.

state
​string · enum

Filter by an Offering's state.

The state of this offering:

  • concept: The offering is still in development and not yet available for students
  • cancelled: The offering has been cancelled and is no longer available
  • active: The offering is currently available for students to enrol in and participate
  • inactive: The offering is not currently available for students, but may be available in the future

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
concept
cancelled
active
inactive
resultExpected
​boolean

Filter by resultExpected

since
​string · date-time

Filter all offerings by providing a minimum start moment (dateTime) for the corresponding academic session, RFC3339 (full-date). By default only future offerings are shown (equal to ?since=<today>).

until
​string · date-time

Filter all offerings by providing a maximum end moment (dateTime) for the corresponding academic session, RFC3339 (full-date).

GET /organisations/{organisationId}/programme-offerings › Responses

OK

pageSize
​integer · int32 · required

The number of items per page

pageNumber
​integer · int32 · min: 1 · required

The current page number

hasPreviousPage
​boolean · required

Whether there is a previous page

hasNextPage
​boolean · required

Whether there is a previous page

​object[] · required
totalPages
​integer | null · int32

Total number of pages

ext
​object

Object for additional non-standard attributes

GET/organisations/{organisationId}/programme-offerings
curl --request GET \ --url http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6/organisations/:organisationId/programme-offerings
shell
Example Responses
{ "pageSize": 10, "pageNumber": 1, "hasPreviousPage": false, "hasNextPage": true, "totalPages": 8, "items": [ { "programmeOfferingId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-134564174000", "primaryCode": { "studyLoad": [], "qualificationAwarded": "none", "qualificationDesignations": [], "modeOfStudy": "full_time", "modesOfDelivery": [], "duration": "P1DT10H30M", "firstStartDateTime": "2025-08-28T08:30:00+01:00", "levelOfQualification": "eqf_6", "level": "master", "fieldsOfStudy": "0732", "enrolment": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "enrolment through SIS. [The limited implementation of Git Hub Markdown syntax](https://openonderwijsapi.nl/v6.0/#/technical/formatting-text) MAY be used for rich text representation." } ], "resources": [ "book to be announced", "on-line resource x" ], "learningOutcomeIds": [], "learningOutcomes": [], "assessment": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Exam on campus" } ], "admissionRequirements": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Students need to be enrolled at qualifying institutions of higher education that participate in this alliance" } ], "qualificationRequirements": [], "formalDocument": "diploma", "link": "https://bijvak.nl", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "crohoCreboCode", "code": "59312" } ], "addresses": [], "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [], "children": [], "coordinatorIds": [], "coordinators": [], "instructorIds": [], "instructors": [], "organisationId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "organisation": { "organisationId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-123514174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "organisationType": "root", "name": [ { "language": "nl-NL", "value": "Coöperatie SURF U.A." } ], "shortName": "SURF", "description": [ { "language": "nl-NL", "value": "SURF is een coöperatieve vereniging van Nederlandse onderwijs- en onderzoeksinstellingen waarin de leden hun krachten bundelen. De leden zijn eigenaar van SURF." } ], "addresses": [], "link": "https://surf.nl", "logo": "https://www.surf.nl/themes/surf/logo.svg", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "institution_code", "code": "114B529" }, { "codeType": "kvk_organisation_id", "code": "50277374" } ], "rootId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [], "children": [] }, "supplementaryInformation": [], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "validFrom": "2025-09-01T09:00:00+01:00", "validTo": "2025-09-01T09:00:00+01:00" }, "organisationId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "organisation": { "organisationId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-123514174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "organisationType": "root", "name": [ { "language": "nl-NL", "value": "Coöperatie SURF U.A." } ], "shortName": "SURF", "description": [ { "language": "nl-NL", "value": "SURF is een coöperatieve vereniging van Nederlandse onderwijs- en onderzoeksinstellingen waarin de leden hun krachten bundelen. De leden zijn eigenaar van SURF." } ], "addresses": [], "link": "https://surf.nl", "logo": "https://www.surf.nl/themes/surf/logo.svg", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "institution_code", "code": "114B529" }, { "codeType": "kvk_organisation_id", "code": "50277374" } ], "rootId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [], "children": [], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" } } } ] }
json
application/json

GET /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}

GET
http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6
/programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}

Get a single programme offering.

GET /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › path Parameters

programmeOfferingId
​string · uuid · required

Programme Offering ID

GET /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › query Parameters

expand
​string[] · style: form

Optional properties to expand, separated by a comma

Enum values:
academic_session
course
course_offering
learning_component
organisation
programme
programme_offering
test_component
fields
​string · style: form

Allows clients to indicate which fields should be included in the response.
This parameter supports the principle of data minimisation and helps to optimise data usage and performance by reducing unnecessary data transmission.

The fields parameter uses nested field selection syntax with parentheses for subfields, for example: programme(code) or campus(city).
Multiple fields can be grouped within parentheses, for example:
fields=(id,title,ectsCredits,programme(code),campus(city)).

When omitted, the server returns all fields the client has access to.
Unknown field names SHOULD be ignored.
The server MUST always include mandatory fields (e.g., identifiers such as id) that are required for a valid or minimal response, even if not explicitly requested.

Important: This is a request hint, not a security feature.
The server MAY disregard the request for a restricted set of fields, and the final response structure MAY depend on server logic and the client’s access rights.

If a client requests unauthorised fields, these MUST be silently omitted or redacted.

consumer
​string

Request entities intended for a specific consumer. The consumer profile allows for adding additional data, or specific rules concerning the presentation of the data. A consumer can be selected based on the key of the consumer profile. An implementation of the OEAPI SHOULD always return the consumer information inside the consumer property of the object(s) that are requested. Further information regarding the use of consumers can be found in the documentation

GET /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › Responses

OK

A programme offering which describes the programme in time.
programmeOfferingId
​string · uuid · required

The unique ID of the programme offering, this should be unique across all programme, course, learning, and test component offerings.

​object · required
​object[] · minItems: 1 · required

The name of this offering

​

The offering identifiers (0..N) associated with this group.

academicSessionId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A named period of time that can be used to communicate the various schedules and time periods an institution recognizes and uses to organise their education. AcademicSessions can be nested. Offerings MAY be linked to a specific AcademicSession to indicate that the specified Offering takes place during the AcademicSession, however this is not mandatory.

​

The state of this offering, e.g. active, inactive, archived

​

The rostering state of this offering indicating the state in relation to planning, e.g. active, inactive, archived

abbreviation
​string | null · maxLength: 256

The abbreviation or internal code used to identify this offering

​array | null · minItems: 1

The description of this offering.

teachingLanguages
​array | null · minItems: 1

The languages in which this course is given, should at least a two-letter language code as specified by RFC 4647. A student should be reasonably proficient in each language to be able to follow the offering.

modesOfDelivery
​array | null

The mode of delivery of the component, based on the EU vocabulary:
https://op.europa.eu/en/web/eu-vocabularies/dataset/-/resource?uri=http://publications.europa.eu/resource/dataset/learning-assessment

CodeDescription
blendedStructured combination of online and in-person learning
coilCollaborative Online International Learning; joint, cross-
institutional, online delivery (virtual exchange /
co-taught across institutions)
hybridDelivery using different modes in a flexible and
interchangeable way
joint_deliveryProgramme delivered collaboratively by two or more
institutions (national or international), with shared
responsibility for curriculum and teaching
onlineReal-time learning delivered entirely via the internet
presentialLearning that takes place in a physical classroom setting
project_basedLearning or assessment conducted as part of a project team
research_lab_basedLearning that occurs within a research environment
work_basedLearning through practical work or workplace experience

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
blended
coil
hybrid
joint_delivery
online
presential
project_based
research_lab_based
maxNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The maximum number of students allowed to enrol for this offering

enrolledNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The number of students who have already enrolled for this offering

pendingNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The number of students who have a pending enrolment request for this offering

minNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The minimum number of students needed for this offering to proceed

resultExpected
​boolean | null

resultExpected, previously known as isLineItem is used so the specific instance of the object is identified as being an element that CAN contain “grade” information. Offerings need not always result in a grade or another type of result. If there is a result expected from a programmeOffering/courseOffering/componentOffering the is resultExpected field should be set to true

​
link
​string | null · uri · maxLength: 2048

URL of this offering's webpage.

​array | null

An array of additional human readable codes/identifiers for the entity being described.

​array | null

An array of periods that a person can enrol into this offering. The period is defined by target group and dateTime

​object[]

Optional supplementary information associated with this resource.

​
​
startDateTime
​string | null · date-time

The moment on which this offering starts, RFC3339 (date-time)

endDateTime
​string | null · date-time

The moment on which this offering ends, RFC3339 (date-time)

flexibleEntryPeriodStartDateTime
​string | null · date-time

Use this attribute for courses that allow participants who have already enrolled to begin their participation at different moments without missing essential content. This attribute MUST be used in combination with flexibleEntryPeriodEndDateTime.

flexibleEntryPeriodEndDateTime
​string | null · date-time

If this is a course wherein participants who have enrolled can start at various moments without missing anything, use this attribute in combination with flexibleEntryPeriodStartDateTime.

​array | null

Addresses for this offering

​array | null · minItems: 1

Price information for this offering.

programmeId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A collection of courses that lead to a certifiable learning outcome

organisationId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A description of a group of people working together to achieve a goal

GET/programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}
curl --request GET \ --url http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6/programme-offerings/:programmeOfferingId
shell
Example Responses
{ "programmeOfferingId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-134564174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "groupIds": [], "academicSessionId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "academicSession": { "academicSessionId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "academicSessionType": "semester", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Autumn term 2020" } ], "abbreviation": "SPRING-2026", "startDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "endDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [], "children": [], "yearId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "otherCodes": [], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" } }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Final written test for INFOMQNM for fall semester 2020" } ], "state": "active", "rosteringState": "definitive", "abbreviation": "Test-INFOMQNM-20FS", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of ­­­statistics, including:\nHandle hypothesis testing with complex designs (e.g., including , dependent, independent, and co variates)\nData preparation (e.g., coding and feature selection)\nReason towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": [], "modesOfDelivery": [], "maxNumberStudents": 200, "enrolledNumberStudents": 150, "pendingNumberStudents": 50, "minNumberStudents": 15, "resultExpected": true, json
application/json

PUT /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}

PUT
http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6
/programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}

Update all attributes of a single programme offering.

PUT /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › path Parameters

programmeOfferingId
​string · uuid · required

Programme Offering ID

PUT /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › Request Body

A programme offering which describes the programme in time.
programmeOfferingId
​string · uuid · required

The unique ID of the programme offering, this should be unique across all programme, course, learning, and test component offerings.

​object · required
​object[] · minItems: 1 · required

The name of this offering

​

The offering identifiers (0..N) associated with this group.

academicSessionId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A named period of time that can be used to communicate the various schedules and time periods an institution recognizes and uses to organise their education. AcademicSessions can be nested. Offerings MAY be linked to a specific AcademicSession to indicate that the specified Offering takes place during the AcademicSession, however this is not mandatory.

​

The state of this offering, e.g. active, inactive, archived

​

The rostering state of this offering indicating the state in relation to planning, e.g. active, inactive, archived

abbreviation
​string | null · maxLength: 256

The abbreviation or internal code used to identify this offering

​array | null · minItems: 1

The description of this offering.

teachingLanguages
​array | null · minItems: 1

The languages in which this course is given, should at least a two-letter language code as specified by RFC 4647. A student should be reasonably proficient in each language to be able to follow the offering.

modesOfDelivery
​array | null

The mode of delivery of the component, based on the EU vocabulary:
https://op.europa.eu/en/web/eu-vocabularies/dataset/-/resource?uri=http://publications.europa.eu/resource/dataset/learning-assessment

CodeDescription
blendedStructured combination of online and in-person learning
coilCollaborative Online International Learning; joint, cross-
institutional, online delivery (virtual exchange /
co-taught across institutions)
hybridDelivery using different modes in a flexible and
interchangeable way
joint_deliveryProgramme delivered collaboratively by two or more
institutions (national or international), with shared
responsibility for curriculum and teaching
onlineReal-time learning delivered entirely via the internet
presentialLearning that takes place in a physical classroom setting
project_basedLearning or assessment conducted as part of a project team
research_lab_basedLearning that occurs within a research environment
work_basedLearning through practical work or workplace experience

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
blended
coil
hybrid
joint_delivery
online
presential
project_based
research_lab_based
maxNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The maximum number of students allowed to enrol for this offering

enrolledNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The number of students who have already enrolled for this offering

pendingNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The number of students who have a pending enrolment request for this offering

minNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The minimum number of students needed for this offering to proceed

resultExpected
​boolean | null

resultExpected, previously known as isLineItem is used so the specific instance of the object is identified as being an element that CAN contain “grade” information. Offerings need not always result in a grade or another type of result. If there is a result expected from a programmeOffering/courseOffering/componentOffering the is resultExpected field should be set to true

​
link
​string | null · uri · maxLength: 2048

URL of this offering's webpage.

​array | null

An array of additional human readable codes/identifiers for the entity being described.

​array | null

An array of periods that a person can enrol into this offering. The period is defined by target group and dateTime

​object[]

Optional supplementary information associated with this resource.

​
​
startDateTime
​string | null · date-time

The moment on which this offering starts, RFC3339 (date-time)

endDateTime
​string | null · date-time

The moment on which this offering ends, RFC3339 (date-time)

flexibleEntryPeriodStartDateTime
​string | null · date-time

Use this attribute for courses that allow participants who have already enrolled to begin their participation at different moments without missing essential content. This attribute MUST be used in combination with flexibleEntryPeriodEndDateTime.

flexibleEntryPeriodEndDateTime
​string | null · date-time

If this is a course wherein participants who have enrolled can start at various moments without missing anything, use this attribute in combination with flexibleEntryPeriodStartDateTime.

​array | null

Addresses for this offering

​array | null · minItems: 1

Price information for this offering.

programmeId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A collection of courses that lead to a certifiable learning outcome

organisationId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A description of a group of people working together to achieve a goal

PUT /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › Responses

OK

No data returned
PUT/programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}
curl --request PUT \ --url http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6/programme-offerings/:programmeOfferingId \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data @- <<EOF { "programmeOfferingId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-134564174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "groupIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "academicSessionId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "academicSession": { "academicSessionId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "academicSessionType": "semester", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Autumn term 2020" } ], "abbreviation": "SPRING-2026", "startDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "endDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "children": [ null ], "yearId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" } ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Final written test for INFOMQNM for fall semester 2020" } ], "state": "active", "rosteringState": "definitive", "abbreviation": "Test-INFOMQNM-20FS", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of ­­­statistics, including:\nHandle hypothesis testing with complex designs (e.g., including , dependent, independent, and co variates)\nData preparation (e.g., coding and feature selection)\nReason towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": Relate opinions as voiced collectively or individually by:\n * animal protection societies\n * patient support societies\n * establishments and researchers\n * industry, including pharma, biotech and food\n * people with personal, cultural or religious beliefs;\n2. Recognize the fundamental right of freedom of speech;\n3. Recall different perspectives which enable an individual to\n determine their own opinion on the use of animals for scientific\n purposes;\n4. Explain how different perspectives drive forward advancements\n in animal welfare, legislation and science.\n(based on: https://repub.eur.nl/pub/132564/Repub_132564_O-A.pdf)\n" } ], "parentIds": [ "endDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "children": [ null ], "yearId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" } ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Final written test for INFOMQNM for fall semester 2020" } ], "state": "active", "rosteringState": "definitive", "abbreviation": "Test-INFOMQNM-20FS", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of ­­­statistics, including:\nHandle hypothesis testing with complex designs (e.g., including , dependent, independent, and co variates)\nData preparation (e.g., coding and feature selection)\nReason towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": json
Example Responses
No example specified for this content type

PATCH /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}

PATCH
http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6
/programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}

Change attributes of a single programme offering.

PATCH /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › path Parameters

programmeOfferingId
​string · uuid · required

Programme Offering ID

PATCH /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › Request Body

A programme offering which describes the programme in time.
programmeOfferingId
​string · uuid · required

The unique ID of the programme offering, this should be unique across all programme, course, learning, and test component offerings.

​object · required
​object[] · minItems: 1 · required

The name of this offering

​

The offering identifiers (0..N) associated with this group.

academicSessionId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A named period of time that can be used to communicate the various schedules and time periods an institution recognizes and uses to organise their education. AcademicSessions can be nested. Offerings MAY be linked to a specific AcademicSession to indicate that the specified Offering takes place during the AcademicSession, however this is not mandatory.

​

The state of this offering, e.g. active, inactive, archived

​

The rostering state of this offering indicating the state in relation to planning, e.g. active, inactive, archived

abbreviation
​string | null · maxLength: 256

The abbreviation or internal code used to identify this offering

​array | null · minItems: 1

The description of this offering.

teachingLanguages
​array | null · minItems: 1

The languages in which this course is given, should at least a two-letter language code as specified by RFC 4647. A student should be reasonably proficient in each language to be able to follow the offering.

modesOfDelivery
​array | null

The mode of delivery of the component, based on the EU vocabulary:
https://op.europa.eu/en/web/eu-vocabularies/dataset/-/resource?uri=http://publications.europa.eu/resource/dataset/learning-assessment

CodeDescription
blendedStructured combination of online and in-person learning
coilCollaborative Online International Learning; joint, cross-
institutional, online delivery (virtual exchange /
co-taught across institutions)
hybridDelivery using different modes in a flexible and
interchangeable way
joint_deliveryProgramme delivered collaboratively by two or more
institutions (national or international), with shared
responsibility for curriculum and teaching
onlineReal-time learning delivered entirely via the internet
presentialLearning that takes place in a physical classroom setting
project_basedLearning or assessment conducted as part of a project team
research_lab_basedLearning that occurs within a research environment
work_basedLearning through practical work or workplace experience

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
blended
coil
hybrid
joint_delivery
online
presential
project_based
research_lab_based
maxNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The maximum number of students allowed to enrol for this offering

enrolledNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The number of students who have already enrolled for this offering

pendingNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The number of students who have a pending enrolment request for this offering

minNumberStudents
​number | null · int32 · min: 0

The minimum number of students needed for this offering to proceed

resultExpected
​boolean | null

resultExpected, previously known as isLineItem is used so the specific instance of the object is identified as being an element that CAN contain “grade” information. Offerings need not always result in a grade or another type of result. If there is a result expected from a programmeOffering/courseOffering/componentOffering the is resultExpected field should be set to true

​
link
​string | null · uri · maxLength: 2048

URL of this offering's webpage.

​array | null

An array of additional human readable codes/identifiers for the entity being described.

​array | null

An array of periods that a person can enrol into this offering. The period is defined by target group and dateTime

​object[]

Optional supplementary information associated with this resource.

​
​
startDateTime
​string | null · date-time

The moment on which this offering starts, RFC3339 (date-time)

endDateTime
​string | null · date-time

The moment on which this offering ends, RFC3339 (date-time)

flexibleEntryPeriodStartDateTime
​string | null · date-time

Use this attribute for courses that allow participants who have already enrolled to begin their participation at different moments without missing essential content. This attribute MUST be used in combination with flexibleEntryPeriodEndDateTime.

flexibleEntryPeriodEndDateTime
​string | null · date-time

If this is a course wherein participants who have enrolled can start at various moments without missing anything, use this attribute in combination with flexibleEntryPeriodStartDateTime.

​array | null

Addresses for this offering

​array | null · minItems: 1

Price information for this offering.

programmeId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A collection of courses that lead to a certifiable learning outcome

organisationId
​string · uuid

An identifier of another resource.

​object

A description of a group of people working together to achieve a goal

PATCH /programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId} › Responses

OK

No data returned
PATCH/programme-offerings/{programmeOfferingId}
curl --request PATCH \ --url http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6/programme-offerings/:programmeOfferingId \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data @- <<EOF { "programmeOfferingId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-134564174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "groupIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "academicSessionId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "academicSession": { "academicSessionId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "academicSessionType": "semester", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Autumn term 2020" } ], "abbreviation": "SPRING-2026", "startDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "endDateTime": "2025-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "children": [ null ], "yearId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" } ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Final written test for INFOMQNM for fall semester 2020" } ], "state": "active", "rosteringState": "definitive", "abbreviation": "Test-INFOMQNM-20FS", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of ­­­statistics, including:\nHandle hypothesis testing with complex designs (e.g., including , dependent, independent, and co variates)\nData preparation (e.g., coding and feature selection)\nReason towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": [ "en-GB" ], "modesOfDelivery": [ "blended" ], "maxNumberStudents": 200, "enrolledNumberStudents": 150, "pendingNumberStudents": 50, "minNumberStudents": 15, "resultExpected": true, "resultValueType": "grade_0_10", "link": "https://osiris.uu.nl/osiris_student_uuprd/OnderwijsCatalogusZoekCursus.do#submitForm?cursuscode=INFOMQNM", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" } ], "enrolmentPeriods": [ { "startDateTime": "2020-09-28T08:30:00+01:00", "endDateTime": "2020-09-30T20:00:00+01:00", "targetGroups": [ "string" ], "enrolmentType": "url", "enrolmentUrl": "https://university.example.org/", "queueEnabled": false, "queuedNumberStudents": 200, "maxQueuedNumberStudents": 200, "comment": "Additional information...", "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} } ], "supplementaryInformation": [ { "role": "badge", "type": "text_plain", "value": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "programme that is a place holder for all courses that are made available for student mobility" } ] } ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "programmeType": "programme", "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Biology" } ], "abbreviation": "BIO", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "The study of life" } ], "teachingLanguages": [ "en-GB" ], "studyLoad": [ { "studyLoadUnit": "ects", "value": 3 } ], "qualificationAwarded": "none", "qualificationDesignations": [ "string" ], "modeOfStudy": "full_time", "modesOfDelivery": [ "blended" ], "duration": "P1DT10H30M", "firstStartDateTime": "2025-08-28T08:30:00+01:00", "levelOfQualification": "eqf_6", "level": "master", "fieldsOfStudy": "0732", "enrolment": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "enrolment through SIS. [The limited implementation of Git Hub Markdown syntax](https://openonderwijsapi.nl/v6.0/#/technical/formatting-text) MAY be used for rich text representation." } ], "resources": [ "book to be announced", "on-line resource x" ], "learningOutcomeIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "learningOutcomes": [ { "learningOutcomeId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Describe the differing views, within society, relating to the scientific uses of animals and recognize the need to respect these.\n" } ], "abbreviation": "LO12_INFO_RET", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "The candidate should have retained the information that they\nhave been taught and be able to:\n1. Relate opinions as voiced collectively or individually by:\n * animal protection societies\n * patient support societies\n * establishments and researchers\n * industry, including pharma, biotech and food\n * people with personal, cultural or religious beliefs;\n2. Recognize the fundamental right of freedom of speech;\n3. Recall different perspectives which enable an individual to\n determine their own opinion on the use of animals for scientific\n purposes;\n4. Explain how different perspectives drive forward advancements\n in animal welfare, legislation and science.\n(based on: https://repub.eur.nl/pub/132564/Repub_132564_O-A.pdf)\n" } ], "parentIds": [ "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Final written test for INFOMQNM for fall semester 2020" } ], "state": "active", "rosteringState": "definitive", "abbreviation": "Test-INFOMQNM-20FS", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of ­­­statistics, including:\nHandle hypothesis testing with complex designs (e.g., including , dependent, independent, and co variates)\nData preparation (e.g., coding and feature selection)\nReason towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": [The limited implementation of Git Hub Markdown syntax](https://openonderwijsapi.nl/v6.0/#/technical/formatting-text) MAY be used for rich text representation." } ], "resources": [ "book to be announced", "on-line resource x" ], "learningOutcomeIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "learningOutcomes": [ { "learningOutcomeId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "name": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "Describe the differing views, within society, relating to the scientific uses of animals and recognize the need to respect these.\n" } ], "abbreviation": "LO12_INFO_RET", "description": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "The candidate should have retained the information that they\nhave been taught and be able to:\n1. Relate opinions as voiced collectively or individually by:\n * animal protection societies\n * patient support societies\n * establishments and researchers\n * industry, including pharma, biotech and food\n * people with personal, cultural or religious beliefs;\n2. Recognize the fundamental right of freedom of speech;\n3. Recall different perspectives which enable an individual to\n determine their own opinion on the use of animals for scientific\n purposes;\n4. Explain how different perspectives drive forward advancements\n in animal welfare, legislation and science.\n(based on: https://repub.eur.nl/pub/132564/Repub_132564_O-A.pdf)\n" } ], "parentIds": [ De leden zijn eigenaar van SURF." } ], "addresses": [ { "addressType": "postal", "street": "Moreelsepark", "streetNumber": "48", "additional": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "On the other side of the road" } ], "postCode": "3511 EP", "city": "Utrecht", "countryCode": { "iso3166-1-alpha2": "NL", "iso3166-1-alpha3": "NLD", "iso3166-2": "BQ-BO", "iso3166-3": "ANHH" }, "geolocation": { "latitude": 52.089123, "longitude": 5.113337 }, "ext": {} } ], "link": "https://surf.nl", "logo": "https://www.surf.nl/themes/surf/logo.svg", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "institution_code", "code": "114B529" }, { "codeType": "kvk_organisation_id", "code": "50277374" } ], "rootId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "children": [ null ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} }, "supplementaryInformation": [ { "role": "badge", "type": "text_plain", "value": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "programme that is a place holder for all courses that are made available for student mobility" } ] } ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {}, "validFrom": "2025-09-01T09:00:00+01:00", "validTo": "2025-09-01T09:00:00+01:00" }, "organisationId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "organisation": { "organisationId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-123514174000", "primaryCode": { "codeType": "identifier", "code": "1234qwe12" }, "organisationType": "root", "name": [ { "language": "nl-NL", "value": "Coöperatie SURF U.A." } ], "shortName": "SURF", "description": [ { "language": "nl-NL", "value": "SURF is een coöperatieve vereniging van Nederlandse onderwijs- en onderzoeksinstellingen waarin de leden hun krachten bundelen. De leden zijn eigenaar van SURF." } ], "addresses": [ { "addressType": "postal", "street": "Moreelsepark", "streetNumber": "48", "additional": [ { "language": "en-GB", "value": "On the other side of the road" } ], "postCode": "3511 EP", "city": "Utrecht", "countryCode": { "iso3166-1-alpha2": "NL", "iso3166-1-alpha3": "NLD", "iso3166-2": "BQ-BO", "iso3166-3": "ANHH" }, "geolocation": { "latitude": 52.089123, "longitude": 5.113337 }, "ext": {} } ], "link": "https://surf.nl", "logo": "https://www.surf.nl/themes/surf/logo.svg", "otherCodes": [ { "codeType": "institution_code", "code": "114B529" }, { "codeType": "kvk_organisation_id", "code": "50277374" } ], "rootId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "parentId": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "childIds": [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" ], "children": [ null ], "consumer": { "consumerKey": "test-consumer", "exampleProperty": "value-of-example-property" }, "ext": {} } }
json
Example Responses
No example specified for this content type

GET /programmes/{programmeId}/programme-offerings

GET
http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6
/programmes/{programmeId}/programme-offerings

Get a list of all offerings for this programme, ordered chronologically.

GET /programmes/{programmeId}/programme-offerings › path Parameters

programmeId
​string · uuid · required

Programme ID

GET /programmes/{programmeId}/programme-offerings › query Parameters

pageSize
​integer · enum · int32

The number of items per page

Enum values:
10
20
50
100
250
Default: 10
pageNumber
​integer · int32 · min: 1

The page number to get. Page numbers start at 1.

consumer
​string

Request entities intended for a specific consumer. The consumer profile allows for adding additional data, or specific rules concerning the presentation of the data. A consumer can be selected based on the key of the consumer profile. An implementation of the OEAPI SHOULD always return the consumer information inside the consumer property of the object(s) that are requested. Further information regarding the use of consumers can be found in the documentation

​object · style: deepObject · explode: true

Filter object serialised as filter_query[field][operation]=value. Multiple top-level fields are combined with AND. CSV is accepted where noted.

Inspired by Storyblok (https://www.storyblok.com/docs/api/content-delivery/v2/filter-queries)

Wildcards:

  • Prefer using only the asterisk * as a wildcard for partial matches (e.g., like and not_like).

Implementation note:

  • The availability and behaviour of this query functionality are entirely determined by the organisation hosting the API implementation. It is not mandatory for implementers to support this functionality, and it cannot be enforced upon organisations that provide or consume OEAPI endpoints.
  • It is up to each implementer to decide whether to support this feature. It is not a requirement of the OEAPI standard itself.
  • Consumers or working groups that wish to apply specific filtering mechanisms are encouraged to do so using this approach for the sake of consistency across implementations.

Examples:

  • Filter course offerings by programme, delivery, language and start date
    filter_query[programme.code][in]=B-IT-2025&filter_query[organisation.code][in]=RuG&filter_query[mode_of_delivery][in]=on_campus,hybrid&filter_query[language_of_instruction][in]=en-GB&filter_query[start_date][gt_date]=2025-09-01T00:00:00Z

  • Only offerings with email contact present
    filter_query[contacts.email][is]=not_empty

​object[] · style: deepObject · explode: true

OR blocks. Provide an array of single-field filter objects, each combined with OR. Serialises as filter_query[__or][][field][operation]=value.

Inspired by Storyblok (https://www.storyblok.com/docs/api/content-delivery/v2/filter-queries)

Wildcards:

  • Prefer using only the asterisk * as a wildcard for partial matches (e.g., like).

Implementation note:

  • The availability and behaviour of this query functionality are entirely determined by the organisation hosting the API implementation. It is not mandatory for implementers to support this functionality, and it cannot be enforced upon organisations that provide or consume OEAPI endpoints.
  • It is up to each implementer to decide whether to support this feature. It is not a requirement of the OEAPI standard itself.
  • Consumers or working groups that wish to apply specific filtering mechanisms are encouraged to do so using this approach for the sake of consistency across implementations.

Examples:

  • Provider is Org A or Org B, OR campus city contains “Utrecht”
    ?filter_query[__or][][organisation.id][in]=org-uu,org-hku&filter_query[__or][][campus.city][like]=*Utrecht*

  • Start date after 1 Sept 2025 OR has evening/block_week tag
    ?filter_query[__or][][start_date][gt_date]=2025-09-01T00:00:00Z&filter_query[__or][][tags][any_in_array]=evening,block_week

fields
​string · style: form

Allows clients to indicate which fields should be included in the response.
This parameter supports the principle of data minimisation and helps to optimise data usage and performance by reducing unnecessary data transmission.

The fields parameter uses nested field selection syntax with parentheses for subfields, for example: programme(code) or campus(city).
Multiple fields can be grouped within parentheses, for example:
fields=(id,title,ectsCredits,programme(code),campus(city)).

When omitted, the server returns all fields the client has access to.
Unknown field names SHOULD be ignored.
The server MUST always include mandatory fields (e.g., identifiers such as id) that are required for a valid or minimal response, even if not explicitly requested.

Important: This is a request hint, not a security feature.
The server MAY disregard the request for a restricted set of fields, and the final response structure MAY depend on server logic and the client’s access rights.

If a client requests unauthorised fields, these MUST be silently omitted or redacted.

q
​string

Filter by items having a name, abbreviation or description containing the given search term (exact partial match, case insensitive)

teachingLanguage
​string · minLength: 2 · pattern: ^([a-z]{2,3})(-([A-Z…

Filter by teachingLanguage, which is a string describing the main teaching language, should be at least a two-letter language code as specified by ISO 6467.

state
​string · enum

Filter by an Offering's state.

The state of this offering:

  • concept: The offering is still in development and not yet available for students
  • cancelled: The offering has been cancelled and is no longer available
  • active: The offering is currently available for students to enrol in and participate
  • inactive: The offering is not currently available for students, but may be available in the future

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
concept
cancelled
active
inactive
modeOfStudy
​string · enum

Filter by modeOfStudy

Indicates the mode of study: full-time, part-time, dual or self-paced.

  • full_time: Standard daytime study schedule
  • part_time: Study scheduled outside regular working hours, such as evenings and weekends
  • dual_training: Combination of workplace learning and academic study
  • self_paced: Student sets their own pace and timing for study

This is an extensible enumeration. Use the prefix x- for custom values.

Enum values:
full_time
part_time
dual_training
self_paced
resultExpected
​boolean

Filter by resultExpected

since
​string · date-time

Filter all offerings by providing a minimum start moment (dateTime) for the corresponding academic session, RFC3339 (full-date). By default only future offerings are shown (equal to ?since=<today>).

until
​string · date-time

Filter all offerings by providing a maximum end moment (dateTime) for the corresponding academic session, RFC3339 (full-date).

GET /programmes/{programmeId}/programme-offerings › Responses

OK

pageSize
​integer · int32 · required

The number of items per page

pageNumber
​integer · int32 · min: 1 · required

The current page number

hasPreviousPage
​boolean · required

Whether there is a previous page

hasNextPage
​boolean · required

Whether there is a previous page

​object[] · required
totalPages
​integer | null · int32

Total number of pages

ext
​object

Object for additional non-standard attributes

GET/programmes/{programmeId}/programme-offerings
curl --request GET \ --url http://demo01.eduapi.nl/v6/programmes/:programmeId/programme-offerings
shell
Example Responses

"language": "en-GB", "value": "'Prove in writing knowledge of research methods, including:\nAcquire knowledge of HCI research paradigms\nAble to design suitable research studies (e.g., choose between within and between subject designs)\nDefine/apply/design metrics and scales\nDefine/produce materials (e.g., stimuli and questionnaires)\nDefine protocols for research studies\nUnderstands and take in account concepts of reliability and validity\nAnalyse and improve methods and analysis of published scientific articles\nAble to deliver scientific reports\nProve in writing knowledge of towards adequate techniques to ensure valid outcomes (e.g., be aware of type I, type II errors)\nSelect an appropriate sampling method (e.g., stratified)\nPerform parametric tests (e.g., repeated measures (M)ANOVA)\nPerform non-parametric tests (e.g., Chi-square, Mann-Whitney, and Kruskal-Wallis)'\n" } ], "teachingLanguages": [], "modesOfDelivery": [], "maxNumberStudents": 200,
